# Category: redis_exposed_port

xargs: "<replace_with_appropriate_value>"   
networks:    
  <network name="" driver=... > <shared-service/useful info - see below for options> 
redis:         # service that defines our Redis container.          `image, ports and depends on what you need (e.g., other services) are specified here   .     
  image: "<insert_dockerhub_or_other_repository>"     // specify the Docker repository/tag where your redis images resides    # this is an optional field which will be automatically used if not provided with a valid docker-image.             xargs,port - <replace_with_appropriate value> 
  ports:      
    <Replace_value> <Replace_Value>        // Replace these values according to your requirement     port/host  1234 (container)   0.0.0.0(Host)   |          # specify the host and container internal bindings are specified here or set as <none>  for not binding any external traffic in this case 
depends_on: - database                         // This service depends on your PostgreSQL DB         if true, it will be started first before other services depending upon that.     True   False |      # specify whether the current container should wait until its dependencies are up and running then start itself; (optional)default false           xargs
postgresql:  &database                         // Define service for your postgres database        Depends on depends_on if set to true, it will be started first before other services depending upon that.     True/False |      # specify whether the current container should wait until its dependencies are up and running then start itself; (optional) default false
  image: "<Insert Docker Hub or Other Repository>" // provide your PostgreSQL docker repository's tag here         this is an optional field which will be automatically used if not provided with a valid postgre_sql-image.     postgresql,12345       # specify the host and container internal bindings are specified above         
  environment:        // set env variables to your application    Replace <Replace Value> where necessary e..g email=user@example      xargs  <replace value>  "<value type>="   , &database, -e PGPASSWORD=$POSTGRES_DB       # specify the pgpassword in an environment variable
  ports:             // Specify your application port here    host machine : container internal    1234 (container)     0.0.0.0(host), xargs  |  PostgreSQL db name, user and password - <replace with appropriate value> PGUSER="<user_name>",PGPASSWORD='$POSTGRES_DB',
